explanation:
Selecting the right water heater size is essential for comfort and energy efficiency. This calculator applies industry-standard sizing based on household occupancy and usage patterns, following IRAM standards and best practices.

## How It's Calculated

Tank capacity is recommended based on family size: 50L for 2 people, 80L for 3, and 120L for 4-5 people. This proven method uses standard ratios for typical residential hot water consumption.

The calculator applies this formula to your inputs and alerts you if values fall outside normal ranges.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a practical reference guide. For critical decisions (major renovations, commercial systems, or specialized needs), consult a licensed plumber or HVAC professional. All values are current for 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: What size water heater do I need for my family?
faq.a: Use the number of people in your household: 50L for 2 people, 80L for 3, and 120L for 4-5 people. Families with higher hot water consumption may need one size larger.

faq.q: How many gallons should my water heater be?
faq.a: A common guideline is 10-15 gallons per person daily. For a family of 4, that's approximately 40-60 gallons or about 150-230 liters.

faq.q: What's the difference between 40, 50, and 80-gallon water heaters?
faq.a: Larger tanks store more hot water for larger households. A 50-gallon (190L) heater suits 3-4 people; an 80-gallon (300L) works for 4-5 people or high-demand homes.

faq.q: Should I choose a traditional tank or tankless water heater?
faq.a: Tank heaters are cheaper upfront; tankless saves energy over time but costs more to install. This calculator helps you size a traditional tank system.
Sizing is practical within ±5-10% accuracy depending on your actual household hot water use, climate, and equipment efficiency. Consult a professional for final specifications.
Yes, all our calculators are free and require no registration, sign-up, or payment.

faq.q: Do you save or collect my personal information?
faq.a: No. All calculations run locally in your browser. We never send data to any external servers.

faq.q: How long does a water heater last?
faq.a: Most water heaters last 8-12 years. Regular maintenance can extend the lifespan. Contact a plumber if you notice rust, leaks, or age-related issues.

faq.q: What does water heater 'recovery rate' mean?
faq.a: Recovery rate (in gallons or liters per hour) measures how quickly your heater can reheat water. Higher recovery rates are important for high-demand households.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, no external data updates required
